original_statement: "[England] won Group L in 67.9% of the sims [from Group L containing Croatia, Ghana and Panama in the 2026 FIFA World Cup]."
statement_topic: FIFA World Cup 2026 - Group stage qualification
statement_context: Excerpt from a comprehensive tournament simulation analysis published by The Analyst. Journalist Andrew Beasley reports the output of the Opta supercomputer (10,000 simulations) published on 1 June 2026.
resolution_criteria: England wins Group L in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, as determined by finishing first in the official FIFA group stage standings published after all group matches conclude on or before 2026-06-26.
outcome_proof: The Opta supercomputer (predictor) gave England a 67.9% chance of winning Group L. Journalist Andrew Beasley (author) published this in The Analyst on 1 June 2026. England finished 1st in Group L with 7 points after the group stage ended on 27 June 2026 according to the final official FIFA group stage standings.
source_text_snippet: Across its 10,000 tournament simulations, England made it through to the knockout stage 96.0% of the time and won Group L in 67.9% of the sims.
